summary: Comprehensive testing of AI-powered traffic accident detection system completed. Backend APIs fully functional, frontend UI working with minor frame display issue. Video source management, detection start/stop, and analytics all operational.

minor_issues.issue: WebSocket frame broadcasting appears functional but video canvas not displaying frames in UI
minor_issues.impact: Live feed shows 'Waiting for video stream...' instead of actual frames

ui_bugs.issue: Video canvas element not found when detection is active
ui_bugs.impact: Live feed shows 'LIVE' status but no actual video frames displayed

integration_issues.issue: Canvas element missing during active detection - frames not rendering in UI
integration_issues.details: Detection starts successfully, WebSocket connects, but video canvas doesn't appear for frame display

passed_tests: ✅ Backend API endpoints (100% pass rate - 11/11 tests)
✅ YouTube video source creation and stream extraction
✅ Settings page navigation and video source management
✅ Analytics stats showing correct active sources count (1 active)
✅ Active alerts panel showing accident history with severity levels
✅ Dashboard loads correctly with stats strip showing 15 accidents
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix video canvas display issue: Live detection starts successfully and shows 'LIVE' status, but video frames are not displaying in the UI canvas. The WebSocket connection works but the canvas element ([data-testid^='video-canvas-']) is not being rendered when detection is active, showing 'Waiting for video stream...' instead of actual frames. This prevents users from seeing the live video feed during detection.

success_percentage.frontend: 85% (UI navigation and detection controls work, but video frame display issue)
